text:
Here are the restoration Steps

*RESTORATION:* 
1. *Validate backups* – Check all saved copies of your data to make sure they’re not infected by the attack. Only use backups from before the attack started.
2. *Wipe infected systems* – Completely erase (format) any computers or servers that were hit by the attack so no harmful files remain.
3. *Rebuild from clean backup* – Use the safe backup you found earlier to put the system back the way it was before the attack.
4. *Re-enable security tools on servers* – Before putting the systems back on the network, reinstall antivirus, firewalls, and other security software so they’re protected.
5. *Reconnect traffic to services and monitor* – Turn the systems back on, let users connect again, and keep a close eye for anything suspicious.

elements.text: Here are the restoration Steps
RESTORATION:
Validate backups
– Check all saved copies of your data to make sure they’re not infected by the attack. Only use backups from before the attack started.
Wipe infected systems
– Completely erase (format) any computers or servers that were hit by the attack so no harmful files remain.
Rebuild from clean backup
– Use the safe backup you found earlier to put the system back the way it was before the attack.
Re-enable security tools on servers
– Before putting the systems back on the network, reinstall antivirus, firewalls, and other security software so they’re protected.
Reconnect traffic to services and monitor
– Turn the systems back on, let users connect again, and keep a close eye for anything suspicious.
